text:
Admission Date:  1994-08-12              Discharge Date:   1987-10-24

Date of Birth:  2015-08-27             Sex:   M

Service: ORTHOPAEDICS

Allergies:
No Known Allergies / Adverse Drug Reactions

Attending:Ashley
Chief Complaint:
Right open femur fracture

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
1994-08-12:  I&D with IM nail Right femur

History of Present Illness:
Mr. Chung is a 61 year old man who was reportedly a motorcycle
driver (unhelmeted)traveling highway speeds and crashed.  He was
taken to St Lukes Hospital Hospital by family who was following his
motorcycle in the back of a pickup truck  At St Lukes Hospital Hospital and
was found to have a right open femur fracture.  He was then
taken by 530 Barr Ranch Apt. 537
Lake Williamfurt, AK 75946-8054 to the Merrick Medical Center for further evaluation and care.


Past Medical History:
Denies

Social History:
N/A

Family History:
N/A

Physical Exam:
Physical Exam on Admission:
Intubated and sedated
RLE in traction.  10cm laceration over anterior thigh above
level
of fracture with debris, no active bleeding.  Thigh compartment
soft.  1+ DP pulse, weaker than L DP.  Babinski negative.
B/L UE, LLE without obvious trauma, no deformity, skin intact.


Pertinent Results:
R Femur XR: comminuted right midshaft femur fracture
- CT head: no intracranial hemorrhage or fracture
- CT C-spine: No cervical spine fracture or malalignment.
- CT Torso: No acute fracture of T/L/S spine. degenerative
changes of L5-S1. Gastric distension despite NG tube. Right
lower lobe consolidation, likely aspiration, though contusion
cannot be excluded.

1987-09-04 06:35AM BLOOD Hct-27.0*
2015-08-23 05:10AM BLOOD Hct-25.0*
2015-11-01 06:10AM BLOOD Glucose-107* UreaN-12 Creat-1.0 Na-135
K-4.2 Cl-103 HCO3-27 AnGap-9

Brief Hospital Course:
  Mr. Chung presented to the Merrick Medical Center on 1994-08-12 via Mclean Southeast
transfer from St Lukes Hospital Hospital after suffering a right open femur
fracture.  He was intubated prior to transfer.  At the Merrick Medical Center he
was evaluated by the trauma, orthopaedic, and vascular surgery.
He was taken to the operating room by orthopaedics and underwent
an I&D with IM nail of his right femur.  He tolerated the
procedure well and was then transferred to the TICU.  He was
weaned and extubated.  He was then transferred out of the TICU
and to the floor.  On the floor he worked with physical therapy
to improve his strength and mobility.  He was transfused with 2
units of packed red blood cells due to acute blood loss anemia.

 The rest of his hospital stay was uneventful with his lab data
and vital signs with his lab data and vital signs within normal
limits and his pain controlled.  He is being discharged today in
stable condition.

Medications on Admission:
none

Discharge Medications:
1. oxycodone-acetaminophen 5-325 mg Tablet Sig: 1-2 Tablets PO
Q4H (every 4 hours) as needed for Pain.
Disp:*100 Tablet(s)* Refills:*0*
2. enoxaparin 40 mg/0.4 mL Syringe Sig: One (1) 40 mg
Subcutaneous QDAILY ().
Disp:*30 40 mg* Refills:*0*
3. Keflex 500 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO four times a
day for 2 weeks.
Disp:*56 Capsule(s)* Refills:*0*


Discharge Disposition:
Home

Discharge Diagnosis:
s/p MCC
Right open femur fracture
Acute blood loss anemia


Discharge Condition:
Mental Status: Clear and coherent.
Level of Consciousness: Alert and interactive.
Activity Status: Ambulatory - requires assistance or aid (walker
or cane).


Discharge Instructions:
Continue to be weightbearing as tolerated on your right leg

Continue your lovenox injections as instructed for a total of 4
weeks after surgery

Please take your pain medication as prescribed

If you have any incrased redness, drainage, or swelling, or if
you have a temperature greater than 101.5, please call the
office or come to the emergency department.

You have been prescribed a narcotic pain medication.  Please
take only as directed and do not drive or operate any machinery
while taking this medication.  There is a 72 hour (Monday
through Friday, 9am to 4pm) response time for prescription refil
requests.  There will be no prescription refils on Saturdays,
Sundays, or holidays.  Please plan accordingly.
Physical Therapy:
Activity:   Activity:  As tolerated
 Right lower extremity:  Full weight bearing

Treatments Frequency:
Staples out 14-21 days after surgery

Followup Instructions:
Please follow up with Kimberly Avila, NP in orthopaedics in 2
weeks, please call 205-389-692 to schedule that appointment.
